description.value:
Grampa Sizzle spends 1 minute telling a short tale about fire, after which one of the PCs must attempt a skill check to act the scene out. If a PC augments the scene by creating a magical fire effect using their own resources, the PC making the skill check gains a +2 status bonus to their check; if they instead attempt to do so with an illusion and Grampa Sizzle disbelieves the illusion with a @Check[perception] check, the associated skill check instead gains a –2 status penalty. All four scenes must be completed in the following order, but which PC attempts which check is up to the party.

  • Scene 1: Grampa Sizzle tells the story of an aristocrat who sets his hat on fire when he tries to light his pipe (@Check[society|dc:24] check or @Check[performance|dc:26] check).

  • Scene 2: Grampa Sizzle tells the story of a goblin who tries to ignite a drunkard's breath, only to get a face full of fire when the drunkard suddenly belches (@Check[stealth|dc:24] check or @Check[thievery|dc:26] check).

  • Scene 3: Grampa Sizzle tells the story of a thief who sneaks into a giant's kitchen through an open window only to land on the giant's red-hot stovetop (@Check[acrobatics|dc:24] check or @Check[athletics|dc:26] check check).

  • Scene 4: Grampa Sizzle tells the story of a bard who flirts with a fiery ifrit genie—awkward romantic shenanigans ensue (@Check[performance|dc:24] check or @Check[diplomacy|dc:26] check). Regardless of the scene, the results of a PC's check are as follows. If a PC takes fire damage, they can defend against it with a @Check[reflex|dc:24|basic] save.

Critical Success The PC depicts the scene with wit, style, and perfect comedic timing. The party earns 2 Laughter Points.

Success Entertainment achieved, but with slight burns. The PC takes @Damage[2d6[fire]] damage, and the party earns 1 Laughter Point.

Failure The PC bungles the attempt and gets a painful and not-so-entertaining burn for their troubles. The PC takes @Damage[4d6[fire]] damage.

Critical Failure Catastrophic explosion or unexpected firestorm! Every PC takes @Damage[8d6[fire]] damage, and the blast frightens the phantasmal crowd, causing the party to lose 1 Laughter Point.

publication.title: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Bastion of Blasphemies
